title: pilosella floribunda subsp. anomelana
intro: Pilosella floribunda subsp. anomelana, also known as the 'Red-Blooming Mouse-Ear', is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone compounds. Pilosella floribunda subsp. anomelana is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting infections, as well as possessing antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various conditions.
